mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions and rising consumer income, which increases corporate earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns positive over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative skew during industrial production contractions (KS test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels above the 235 threshold signal accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >60% of threshold-crossing events over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate cash flow expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns with a classification accuracy above 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in industrial production signals robust manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, which triggers momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of instances following the threshold crossing over a 4-year sample.
